channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Supreme Court turns back challenges to laws keeping abortion opponents away from clinics, patients
item.description: The Supreme Court has declined to hear a pair of cases from abortion opponents who say laws limiting anti-abortion demonstrations near clinics violate their First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The Latest: Federal workers face mass confusion as Musk’s deadline to list accomplishments looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek Monday facing a deadline from President Donald Trump’s cost-cutting chief, Elon Musk, to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: In window before special election, House Republicans push for votes on their agenda
item.description: With a slim majority, Republicans in the Minnesota House are pushing forward bills that are likely not to pass a floor vote. They hope it will still send a political message.
item.dc:creator: Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Ex-Secret Service agent and conservative media personality Dan Bongino picked as FBI deputy director
item.description: A former U.S. Secret Service agent who has penned best-selling books, ran unsuccessfully for office and gained fame as a conservative pundit with TV shows and a popular podcast has been chosen to serve as FBI deputy director.
item.dc:creator: Adriana Gomez Licon And Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump directs ICE agents to find, deport unaccompanied migrant children
item.description: More than 600,000 immigrant children have crossed the U.S.-Mexico border without a parent or legal guardian since 2019.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Musk gives all federal workers 48 hours to explain what they did last week or face consequences
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of federal workers have been given little more than 48 hours to explain what they accomplished over the last week, sparking confusion across key agencies as billionaire Elon Musk expands his drive to slash the size of federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump revels in mass federal firings and jeers at Biden before adoring conservative crowd
item.description: OXON HILL, Md. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Saturday that “nobody's ever seen anything" like his administration's sweeping effort to fire thousands of federal employees and shrink the size of government, congratulating himself for “dominating” Washington and sending bureaucrats “packing.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge largely blocks Trump‘s executive orders ending federal support for DEI programs
item.description: A U.S. district judge granted a preliminary injunction blocking the administration from terminating or changing federal contracts they consider equity-related.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nonprofit working on Mississippi River project loses federal funding
item.description: Mississippi Park Connection said funds for restoration work that has not yet been completed are frozen, as are funds for congressionally funded work that has already been completed.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Here's what to know about Germany's election this Sunday
item.description: An economic slump, an immigration crisis and the lifting of a security blanket provided for decades by the U.S. are issues on the minds of German voters. The far-right AfD is polling in second place.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz, MPR News

item.title: Prosecutors: Presidential pardon for Jan. 6 does not apply to Maryville man's FBI ‘kill list’ plot conviction
item.description: Prosecutors have responded to a motion filed by a Maryville man to dismiss his conviction in to a 2022 plot to murder federal investigators at the FBI Knoxville Field Office after he was pardoned for his role in the January 6, 2021 insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: Judge cancels NYC Mayor Eric Adams' trial and leaves corruption charges intact until mid-March
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A federal judge on Friday canceled the corruption trial for New York City Mayor Eric Adams and appointed counsel to advise the court about the Justice Department’s controversial request to drop charges against the Democrat. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Elon Musk's CPAC talk
item.description: Elon Musk served as one of the main attractions at the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) with a late-scheduled appearance that saw him wield a chainsaw.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Senate Republicans approve budget framework, pushing past Democratic objections after all-night vote
item.description: The early Friday vote was a key step toward providing money the Trump administration says it needs funds for mass deportations and security measures that top the Republican agenda.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Senate adopts budget resolution after marathon overnight vote-a-rama
item.description: The Senate adopted a budget resolution Friday intended to serve as a blueprint to deliver the first part of President Trump’s agenda. Senators voted 52-48, mostly along party lines, on the resolution after a marathon overnight voting session. Sen. Rand Paul (R-Ky.) voted against the measure.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Burchett: Congress to blame if Trump agenda fails
item.description: GOP Tennessee Rep. Tim Burchett tells NewsNation he knows whose fault it will be if Donald Trump's agenda doesn't succeed.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson discourages DOGE stimulus checks idea
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) threw cold water on the idea of using savings from the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) government-slashing activities to send checks to Americans.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Florida sues Target for 'defrauding investors' by selling LGBTQ+ Pride merchandise
item.description: Florida Attorney General James Uthmeier on Thursday announced the state filed a class action lawsuit against Target claiming "the company knowingly misled and defrauded investors by concealing the financial risks of its radical LGBTQ activism.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, Fox 5

item.title: Sen. Mitch McConnell won't seek reelection in 2026, ending long tenure as Republican power broker
item.description: Fellow senators have clapped for Mitch McConnell after he announced he won’t seek reelection next year. The Kentucky Republican is ending a decadeslong tenure as a power broker who championed conservative causes but ultimately ceded ground to the fierce populism of President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump may buy used planes to serve as Air Force One amid Boeing delays
item.description: It's not the planes, rather the heavy modification to make them suitable for the requirements of presidential travel and the top-level security clearances required for those involved, that has added to the cost and delays.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Emotional testimony for 'Jaya's Law' argues for tougher penalties for wrong-way drivers
item.description: A Las Vegas family made an emotional plea to state lawmakers on Tuesday, telling the story of 3-year-old Jaya Brooks, who was killed in a crash involving a wrong-way driver in December 2023.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada may expand revenge porn law to cover AI
item.description: A proposal at the Nevada Legislature would amend the state’s revenge porn law to include computer-generated images.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Tampa Bay sheriffs to advise Florida cabinet on immigration enforcement
item.description: Two Tampa Bay area sheriffs were tapped to join the newly created State Immigration Enforcement Council on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Republican introduces bill to make Trump's birthday a federal holiday
item.description: The Trump’s Birthday and Flag Day Holiday Establishment Act proposes the recognition of both Trump’s birthday and Flag Day, celebrated on June 14, as federal public holidays.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada lawmakers target 'resort fee'-style rent practices, age for gun ownership
item.description: Hidden fees are so out-of-hand that it now costs money just to pay the rent. And it's a good bet that tenants aren't told that until they have signed the lease.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Democrat reintroduces 'fake electors' bill following governor's veto
item.description: Nevada lawmakers will once again debate a bill to penalize a person who creates a false slate of presidential electors -- except this time with lesser penalties.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
